title: Cabinet Extends PMGSY-III Till 2028 With Rs 83,977 Crore — What It Means for Rural India
author: Ashutosh Khulbe
excerpt: The Union Cabinet extended Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana-III till March 2028 with a revised outlay of Rs 83,977 crore. The scheme upgrades rural roads connecting villages to markets, schools, and hospitals.
key_stat: Rs 83,977 crore for 1.25 lakh km rural road upgrades
body: The Union Cabinet on April 18, 2026 approved the continuation of Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana-III (PMGSY-III) beyond March 2025, extending it to March 2028. The revised financial outlay has been increased to Rs 83,977 crore from the earlier Rs 80,250 crore.

PMGSY-III focuses on upgrading 1,25,000 km of rural roads that connect villages to Gramin Agricultural Markets (GrAMs), higher secondary schools, and hospitals. Unlike earlier phases that built new roads to unconnected villages, Phase III strengthens existing roads to handle heavier traffic and all-weather conditions.

The Cabinet also approved extended timelines for different terrain types. Roads and bridges in plain areas must be completed by March 2028. Roads in hilly areas also have a March 2028 deadline, while bridges in hilly regions get an extended deadline until March 2029 due to the difficulty of construction in mountainous terrain.

A significant addition is the sanctioning of 161 Long Span Bridges worth Rs 961 crore. These bridges lie on the alignment of already approved roads but were pending approval. Their construction will improve transport continuity in remote and hilly regions where bridge gaps currently force long detours.

callout.heading: How PMGSY benefits rural families
callout.content: Better roads mean farmers can transport produce to markets faster and at lower cost — directly increasing farm income. Students in rural areas get reliable access to higher secondary schools. Emergency medical transport to hospitals becomes possible in all weather conditions. Independent studies show PMGSY roads have measurably improved rural employment, school enrollment, and healthcare access in connected habitations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between PMGSY-III and PMGSY-IV?
faqs.answer: PMGSY-III (launched 2019) focuses on upgrading existing through routes and major rural links — making roads better, not building new ones. PMGSY-IV (launched 2024) targets providing all-weather road connectivity to remaining unconnected habitations with 500+ population in plain areas and 250+ in hilly/tribal areas. Both are running simultaneously with separate budgets.

faqs.question: How much has PMGSY spent since launch?
faqs.answer: Since its launch in December 2000, PMGSY has built over 8 lakh km of rural roads connecting more than 1.8 lakh habitations across India. The total expenditure across all phases exceeds Rs 3 lakh crore. It is one of the largest rural road development programs in the world.

faqs.question: Can villagers request a PMGSY road for their area?
faqs.answer: PMGSY roads are planned at the district level through District Rural Road Plans. Villagers can raise requests through their Gram Panchayat or elected representatives. The state government and NRRDA prioritize roads based on habitation size, connectivity gaps, and strategic importance. Check the status of roads in your area at omms.nic.in.
